title: Holdings Hub Prototype — Evidence Register
note: Real-world references supporting simulated concepts (PLAN Section 27). Meridian Bank is fictional; real institutions appear as citations only.

entries.claim: Travel Rule data-exchange requirements for VASPs underpin the simulated Travel Rule packet
entries.source: FATF Recommendation 16 / Updated Guidance for a Risk-Based Approach to VAs and VASPs

entries.claim: EU regulatory regime for crypto-assets and stablecoins (CASP licensing, e-money tokens)
Banks exploring tokenised cross-border settlement on unified ledgers
Regulated asset tokenisation pilots across asset classes
Large bank running production blockchain payments/tokenisation platform
Tokenised money market fund with on-chain shares
entries.source: BlackRock BUIDL (BlackRock USD Institutional Digital Liquidity Fund)

entries.claim: Tokenised MMF registered as transfer-agent ledger on public chains
entries.source: Franklin Templeton OnChain U.S. Government Money Fund (BENJI)

entries.claim: Regulated digital-asset exchange and CSD infrastructure operated by a bank group
Fiat-referenced stablecoin with monthly reserve attestations
Euro-denominated e-money token issued under EU rules
Wholesale settlement in tokenised central-bank-money-backed instrument
Multi-bank shared-ledger FX/payments settlement network
Concept of regulated tokenised liabilities across institutions
Distributed ledger repo settlement at scale in production
Collateral mobility via tokenised securities lending
Prudential treatment of banks' cryptoasset exposures
UK regime for cryptoasset registration and financial promotions
International standards for stablecoin arrangements as FMIs
Wholesale CBDC settlement trials with commercial banks
Cross-border multi-CBDC platform experiments
Structured financial messaging standard used for payment simulation fields
Interlinking existing rails with tokenised/CBDC networks
Bank-issued tokenised deposits for corporate treasury payments
entries.source: J.P. Morgan JPM Coin / deposit token work

entries.claim: Continuous 24/7 settlement contrast vs T+1 securities settlement cycles
entries.source: US move to T+1 settlement (SEC rule)

entries.claim: Segregation of duties / four-eyes authorisation as a core bank internal control
entries.source: BCBS — Framework for Internal Control Systems in Banking Organisations

entries.claim: Sanctions/name screening expectations for financial institutions
entries.source: Wolfsberg Group — Guidance on Sanctions Screening

entries.claim: Travel Rule extended to crypto-asset transfers (originator/beneficiary data)
DeFi / unhosted-wallet risk framing supporting the outside-custody-perimeter treatment
entries.source: FATF — Targeted Update on Implementation of the FATF Standards on VAs/VASPs (2023)

entries.claim: Locale-aware number/date/currency formatting via a standard browser API
Free read-only reference crypto prices for the optional live-price overlay
Currency cut-off / settlement-calendar differences between rails (SWIFT vs 24/7)
entries.source: SWIFT gpi payment tracking & cut-off documentation

entries.claim: Browser IndexedDB as the capacity-appropriate store for the Simulation Library
Portable session bundles (ZIP) for export/import round-trips
WCAG 2.1 AA is the accessibility conformance target for the prototype
ARIA roles/states used for dialog, nav landmarks and the diagram grouping
Automated accessibility assertions (axe-core rules) as the CI backstop
Content Security Policy directives constraining script/style/connect origins
Prototype-pollution and injection defences on the untrusted import boundary
entries.source: OWASP — Prototype Pollution Prevention Cheat Sheet

entries.claim: Open-source vulnerability scanning of the dependency lockfile
Baseline application security verification requirements
